story.title: Inventory remaining weakness candidates
story.description: Audit the CC Review plugin at the working-tree revision pinned by baseline-note.md and produce candidate-inventory.md. The deliverable is a candidate table that covers seven scope areas — planning, concurrent workspace mutation, subagent context continuity, structured result enforcement, review integrity, recovery, and UI observability — with each row scored under weakness-ranking-rubric.md and confirmed against current code.

project_file_index.role: Pinned revision + list of already-resolved issues to exclude.
Scoring rubric (five dimensions, weighted aggregation, evidence policy).

definition_of_done.description: candidate-inventory.md exists at repo root and declares its purpose, pins the same revision as baseline-note.md, and cites weakness-ranking-rubric.md.
edge_cases: References baseline-note.md and weakness-ranking-rubric.md by path.
definition_of_done.verify_command: test -f candidate-inventory.md && grep -q 'baseline-note.md' candidate-inventory.md && grep -q 'weakness-ranking-rubric.md' candidate-inventory.md && grep -q '05a5fa3a1ecc76f19ef392e8c0c899a5ddf54c8c' candidate-inventory.md

definition_of_done.description: The inventory covers every one of the seven required scope areas by name: planning, concurrent workspace mutation, subagent context continuity, structured result enforcement, review integrity, recovery, UI observability.
edge_cases: Each scope area appears as a labelled candidate (e.g., 'scope: concurrent workspace mutation').
None of the seven is missing.
definition_of_done.verify_command: for s in 'planning' 'concurrent workspace mutation' 'subagent context continuity' 'structured result enforcement' 'review integrity' 'recovery' 'UI observability'; do grep -qi "$s" candidate-inventory.md || { echo "missing scope: $s" >&2; exit 1; }; done

definition_of_done.description: At least six candidate rows (CAND-01 … CAND-0N) are present, each with the mandatory rubric fields: source_evidence, user_impact, likelihood, recoverability, safety, reach, overall, high_impact, existing test coverage note, and exclusion_check.
edge_cases: Every row includes: source_evidence, user_impact, likelihood, recoverability, safety, reach, overall, high_impact, coverage, exclusion_check.

definition_of_done.description: Every candidate's source_evidence names a file that exists in the current tree.
edge_cases: Every cited path resolves to an existing file.

definition_of_done.description: No candidate re-nominates a resolved R1–R8 issue; the exclusion_check line asserts that on every row.
edge_cases: Every candidate has an exclusion_check line stating 'not in {R1..R8}' (or equivalent).

No candidate title contains 'checkpoint for resume', 'display-state derivation', 'cooperative batch cancellation', 'planner output structural validation', 'de-duplicated execution branches', 'WorkflowRuntime class conversion', 'hasCompletedReview rename', 'first-class batch review result' (the R1–R8 titles).

definition_of_done.description: The overall score of each candidate matches the rubric formula (3·safety + 3·impact + 2·likelihood + 2·recoverability + 1·reach) computed from the row's own per-dimension integers.
edge_cases: Every row's stated `overall: <n>/33` equals the recomputed weighted sum.
high_impact is 'yes' iff overall >= 18 or safety == 3.

definition_of_done.description: Every candidate names at least one existing regression test (file plus test label) OR states 'no direct coverage' with a rationale in its coverage line.
edge_cases: coverage line either contains a tests/… path with a test label, or explicitly says 'no direct coverage'.
When a tests/… path is cited, that file exists.
